author: svlachakis
content:
<@972869819747696693> <@372731338031366165> <@259648573401071617> <@194432762315407360> 

`eth_capabilities` hive rpc-compat fixtures need to be relaxed (speconly)

The eth_capabilities/*.io fixtures ([#755](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-apis/pull/755)) can't work as exact-match tests, because the response is inherently client/config-specific. On the same chain, retention differs per client - e.g. Nethermind reports a 128-block state window and no tx/log window, while geth ([#33886](https://github.com/ethereum/go-ethereum/pull/33886)) reports way more. Only head is client-agnostic.

The current fixtures also don't match any real node - they look hand-copied from geth's `buildCapabilities` unit-test vectors rather than generated (I think there's no testgen generator for the method):
1. head pairs number: 0x13f8e3a with the mainnet genesis hash, but geth derives both from one `CurrentHeader()`, so the pair is always consistent - impossible to produce.
2. `receipts.deleteStrategy`: window, but geth never emits a window for receipts (code and its own test).

Should these be marked speconly (validate against the schema/types) rather than exact-matched? I think exact values can't be portable across clients here.
